# Slowcore


4,712 releases

Characterized by its subdued, often sombre songwriting and slow tempos, while taking primarily from genres like [Dream Pop](https://rateyourmusic.com/genre/dream-pop/) and [Folk Rock](https://rateyourmusic.com/genre/folk-rock/).

Read moreSlowcore is a subgenre of [Indie Rock](https://rateyourmusic.com/genre/indie-rock/) characterized by downbeat melodies, slow tempos, and often minimalist arrangements. The genre was shaped by musical influences like [Folk Rock](https://rateyourmusic.com/genre/folk-rock/), [Contemporary Folk](https://rateyourmusic.com/genre/contemporary-folk/), and later on, [Indie Folk](https://rateyourmusic.com/genre/indie-folk/), creating a subdued atmosphere that clashed against most of the comparatively brash [Alternative Rock](https://rateyourmusic.com/genre/alternative-rock/) that was popular at the time. Foundational artists like [Galaxie 500](https://rateyourmusic.com/artist/galaxie-500) added the atmosphere and songwriting of [Dream Pop](https://rateyourmusic.com/genre/dream-pop/) into the mix, and [Codeine](https://rateyourmusic.com/artist/codeine) would exemplify the influence of [Post-Rock](https://rateyourmusic.com/genre/post-rock/). It also took on the often sloppier-played and lackadaisical songwriting features of its sister scene, [Slacker Rock](https://rateyourmusic.com/genre/slacker-rock/). Some artists additionally incorporated melodic influences from predominantly American forms of guitar-oriented music such as [Country](https://rateyourmusic.com/genre/country/) and [Americana](https://rateyourmusic.com/genre/americana/). Lyrically, the genre can vary, but there is typically a focus on sadness, earning its conflation with 'sadcore', a similar term which applies to a broader style of alternative rock music.

Slowcore started within the late 1980s in the West Coast and East Coast United States, where bands like [American Music Club](https://rateyourmusic.com/artist/american-music-club), Galaxie 500 and [Red House Painters](https://rateyourmusic.com/artist/red-house-painters) set the standards of the genre. [Low](https://rateyourmusic.com/artist/low) would bring the genre to a new audience and begin making slowcore a staple within indie rock in the early 1990s, echoing the message of quiet rebellion against the louder rock of the time, though somewhat deriding the slowcore term. Acts like [Songs: Ohia](https://rateyourmusic.com/artist/songs-ohia) additionally garnered substantial critical acclaim during this period for their fusion of country and folk with slowcore as well. The genre would see a decrease in popularity later in the 2000s, but with the growth of online music discussion into the 2010s, slowcore's following rebounded. 1990s slowcore acts like [Duster](https://rateyourmusic.com/artist/duster) and [Bedhead](https://rateyourmusic.com/artist/bedhead) would garner a newfound appreciation, and newer artists, such as [Giles Corey](https://rateyourmusic.com/artist/giles-corey) and [Horse Jumper of Love](https://rateyourmusic.com/artist/horse-jumper-of-love), made music in the style and accumulated a substantial following from it as well.
